Special Correspondence to The Press


BIRMINGHAM, Ala, March TO-
"Set 'em up again." passed from
the Birmingham vocabulary New
Years eve. That was the eventful
ever when Gambrinus and Bacchus
mounted the water wagon, gloomily
certain the sun would not shine
again. But somehow B'ham, as
sign painters have 1t, continues to
be fine town. The people show
the spirit of energy and enterprise
that marks the live. hustling cities
of the country. They are busy
building up city that, according
to John w. Gates, Is to have 1,000,-
900 population in 1925.


County local option placed the
lid on Birmingham's 1875 saloons.
The city voted wet by 1926 major
ity, but Greater Birmingham went
dry, and the majority against the
saloons in the entire county was
$500.


A striking decrease in the num-
Der of arrests, particularly those
commonly charged to liquor, has
followed the closing of the saloons.
Police Judge N. Is. Feagin found.
when he balanced his books at the
end Of January, that he had passed
upon 504 cases as compared with
779 In January Of last year. Ar
rests for drunkenness had dropped
from TIA In January, 1907, to 33 in
1908; for disorderly conduct the de-
crease was from 80 to Sc; wife
beating decreased 70 per cent; as
sault with intent to commit MUIR
der %; burglary 33, and vagrancy
40 per cent.


Two days before the close of
February. Judge Feagin cast up his
books again and discovered that,
compared with last year, arrests
for intoxlcation fell from 151 to 81
cases; wife beating from Is to 5;
disorderly conduct from sc to 80;
assault and battery So to 20; gam
bling from SO to SO, and assault
with intent to commit murder from
three to none.


The decrease in percentage of
eight most common police court
crimes was Al per cent in January
and SO per cent in February.


Judge Feagin says prohibition Is
success.


Sheriff Higdon reports that the
average Jail population during the
last six months Of 1907 was 300.
This year the average has been
100.


81 will lose 10000 by the change
this year." he says, "but l favor
the closed saloon"
